page: Amazon Web Services r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple categories. The multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, ranks in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, with small-block cached read bandwidth reaching the top 10% tier, and large-block uncached read and write bandwidths placing in the top 25%. Additionally, the processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector extensions and cryptographic acceleration, which contribute to top-tier scores in Passmark CPU tests and Redis SET operations.

From a resource density perspective, the r7a.16xlarge delivers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads due to its fast DDR5 memory and dedicated AMD EPYC cores. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy applications. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, Redis caching layers, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 512.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark evaluations place its multi-core CPU performance, small-block cached memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations in the top 10% tier. It also demonstrates strong performance in Passmark CPU tests and LLM inference. Lacking local storage, it relies on network storage, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large databases, in-memory caching, and data processing tasks that benefit from dedicated compute resources and high-speed DDR5 memory.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 512 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
512.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and cached memory bandwidth performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing
